title: [Q] Can't get systemd running in a CentOS container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Cannot get a systemd-based centos 7 container running

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. 
```
ctr=$(buildah from centos:7)
buildah run $ctr yum install systemd procps-ng -y
buildah config --env container=podman $ctr
buildah config --cmd /sbin/init $ctr
buildah commit $ctr centos-systemd:1
podman run --detach --name alice_gold --tmpfs /tmp --tmpfs /run centos-systemd:1
```

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman exec alice_gold ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  0 15:40 ?        00:00:00 /sbin/init
root         8     0  0 15:40 ?        00:00:00 ps -e -f
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
podman exec bobby_zince ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  0 15:42 ?        00:00:00 /sbin/init
root        18     1  0 15:42 ?        00:00:00 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald
dbus        26     1  0 15:42 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/dbus-daemon --system --address=systemd: --nofork --nopidfile --systemd-activation --syslog-only
root        54     0  0 15:43 ?        00:00:00 ps -e -f
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

* Works with fedora:28 image
* The same pattern works with docker, i.e., systemd is running inside a docker centos container
```
docker exec c7dev_2 ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  1 15:46 ?        00:00:00 /sbin/init
root        18     1  0 15:46 ?        00:00:00 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald
root        33     1  0 15:46 ?        00:00:00 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-logind
dbus        49     1  0 15:46 ?        00:00:00 /bin/dbus-daemon --system --address=systemd: --nofork --nopidfile --systemd-activation
root       110     1  0 15:46 ?        00:00:00 /usr/sbin/sshd -D
root       112     0  0 15:46 ?        00:00:00 ps -ef
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:       0.5.1-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.1
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
# podman info
host:
  MemFree: 22096003072
  MemTotal: 33667489792
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 4.16.5-300.fc28.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 1h 11m 13.35s (Approximately 0.04 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: btrfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Anything in journal?  You do have oci-systemd-hook installed?

@rhatdan yes - in docker the oci-systemd-hook is run.

In podman - if I run as podman privileged container I can see oci-systemd-hook get triggered and even generating an error message
```SELinux is preventing oci-systemd-hoo from using the mac_admin capability```

However if run as a "normal" container nothing seems to happen.
Are you running a centos 6.7 image

Nope - it is a centos:7 image using `$(buildah from centos:7)`. Some systemdhook looks but nothing stands out

```

May 05 08:35:36 localhost.localdomain conmon[26145]: conmon affc9da60a9f9e8c9988 <ninfo>: ctl fifo path: /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/affc9da60a9f9e8c9988601db081ee3f7386019734c773954f540ee400a2a68f/userdata/ctl
May 05 08:35:36 localhost.localdomain conmon[26145]: conmon affc9da60a9f9e8c9988 <ninfo>: terminal_ctrl_fd: 16
```

It works if I update all the stuff inside the container with the CentOS CR RPMs, i.e., the soon-to-be CentOS 7.5 packages!

The difference is

[root@localhost ~]# podman exec bobby_zinc rpm -q systemd
systemd-219-57.el7.x86_64

My guess is the systemd in CentOS/RHEL 7.4 is too old for podman? 

```
# with ps -ef I can see
# podman exec bobby_zinc ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  0 03:15 ?        00:00:00 /sbin/init
root        19     1  0 03:15 ?        00:00:00 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald
dbus        24     1  0 03:15 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/dbus-daemon --system --address=systemd: --nofork --nopidfile --systemd-activation
root        26     1  0 03:15 ?        00:00:00 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-logind
root       151     0  0 03:20 ?        00:00:00 ps -e -f
```

Slightly off-topic: when I do these get-my-base-systemd container running chores, I sometimes end up with the bare /sbin/init - without the journald/dbus-daemon :(

Any advice on how to "bisect" CAP_SYS_ADMIN to figure out what makes systemd stop? I have asked on the systemd list but they usually throw it back to the container runtime :( :(

Closing: seems to be caused by the version of systemd inside the container

Known working: Works with the following systemd-based containers with /sbin/init as cmd/entrypoint:
1. CentOS 7.5 but not CentOS 7.4
2. Fedora 28
